channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: Six MCP tools, one trade: walking an AI agent from RFQ to refund
item.description: Most writing about agent commerce stays at the layer-diagram altitude: rails here, settlement there, trust boundaries in between. Useful, but abstract. Today let's go to ground level and walk through what actually happens - call by call - when an AI agent trades with a stranger and no custodian is...
item.dc:creator: Baris Sozen

item.title: Your MCP Servers Are Burning Tokens Before You Type a Word
item.description: I counted them last week: 47 MCP tools wired into one of my agent sessions. Not called — just loaded. Every tool's full JSON schema, sitting in the system prompt, before I'd typed a single character. I ran the math. Each tool schema (name, description, parameter shapes, examples) averages 150-400...
item.dc:creator: Enjoy Kumawat

item.title: I built a trust firewall for my AI agent's memory — on Cognee's four verbs
item.description: Built for the WeMakeDevs × Cognee hackathon — "The Hangover Part AI: Where's My Context?" AI coding agents are finally getting long-term memory. That's the good news. The bad news is the part nobody likes to say out loud: A memory layer is only as trustworthy as the worst fact in it. The moment an...
item.dc:creator: Himanshu Kumar

item.title: Stop Chunking Your Relationships: Why We Paired a Knowledge Graph with a Vector DB
item.description: If you have spent any time building AI agents for enterprise use cases this year, you have inevitably hit the "RAG Wall." The foundation models (Claude 3.5, GPT-4o) are incredible at reasoning, but they are fundamentally stateless. To fix this, the industry default has been flat semantic RAG: we...
item.dc:creator: Shekhar Kadyan

item.title: What Is AgentGateway? The AI-Native Gateway, Explained for Newbies and Pros
item.description: What Is AgentGateway? The AI-Native Gateway, Explained for Newbies and Pros Spend a week building with AI agents and you hit the same wall I did. The moment there's more than one agent, model, or tool in play, nothing is actually in charge of the traffic moving between them. Two protocols took a...
item.dc:creator: Muralidharan Deenathayalan

item.title: AI Cost Calculator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기
item.description: AI Cost Calculator API — 5분 안에 프로젝트에 통합하기 AI 스타트업, MLOps, LLM 비용 관리 프로젝트에 ai cost calculator 기능이 필요한데 직접 구현하기엔 시간이 아까운가요? REST API 한 줄이면 됩니다. Quick Start # 즉시 테스트 (API 키 불필요) curl https://api.lazy-mac.com/ai-cost/ 실전 예제 Python import requests resp = requests.get("https://api.lazy-mac.com/ai-cost/",...
item.dc:creator: 2x lazymac

item.title: Give your AI agent a cross-venue trading brain in five lines
item.description: Intro Any AI agent that touches markets eventually hits the same wall: it can fetch prices, but it cannot decide. Charts, funding tables, and raw indicators are inputs, not verdicts. Your agent still has to reason its way from "here is the order book" to "should I open a position, hold, or stand...
item.dc:creator: AlgoVault.com

item.title: How to Connect Claude to Your WordPress Site in 5 Minutes
item.description: If you have ever asked Claude to help with WordPress, you know the routine. You describe the problem, Claude writes some code without seeing your actual site, you paste it into WordPress, test it, and half the time something breaks because the AI was guessing about your theme and setup the whole...
item.dc:creator: Siddharth Chudasama

item.title: How to Use MCP Inspector to Build an AI Agent Messaging Workflow
item.description: BridgeXAPI MCP Discovery Lab The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is changing how AI agents interact with software. Instead of hardcoding REST endpoints, AI systems can discover capabilities, inspect tool schemas, plan execution and observe results through a standardized protocol. This hands-on lab...
item.dc:creator: BridgeXAPI

item.title: Gate the Statement, Not the Tool Name
item.description: The original safety gate on the Dolt-over-MCP plugin tried to keep a Claude Code agent harmless by excluding "history-affecting tools" from its MCP grant. It was the wrong granularity, and it did nothing. MCP exposes the entire database through one tool — query / exec — and that tool carries every...
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Longshore

item.title: How to sandbox an MCP server with Docker (--network none is your best friend)
item.description: The single most effective security control for MCP servers is a Docker flag: --network none. When you run an MCP server with no network access, most malicious behavior is neutralized — even if the code is compromised, it can't phone home. Here's how to sandbox an MCP server before trusting it: The...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: MCP Security Checklist: 6 checks before exposing an MCP server
item.description: MCP servers are the new API attack surface. They give LLMs real-world capabilities — filesystem access, database queries, code execution, API calls. But almost none go through a security filter before being exposed. Here's a 6-point checklist I run on every MCP server before trusting it: 1. AUTH —...
item.dc:creator: Edison Flores

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis

item.title: Enterprise-Managed Authorization: Zero-touch OAuth for MCP
item.description: The Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension to the Model Context Protocol is now stable, enabling organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider so users get connected servers on first login without per-app OAuth.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol
